concerns.analysis: There are missing hyperlinks in the output that were clearly present and clickable in the source PDF. The link for 'Levee Terms & Definitions' on Page 4 is entirely missing from the output. Additionally, the hyperlink for 'Division's website.' on Page 5 is completely missing.

The reading order is significantly broken between Page 4 and Page 5. A footnote from the bottom of Page 4 is inserted mid-sentence between the end of the last paragraph on Page 4 and the first paragraph on Page 5, interrupting the continuous flow of the sentence.
concerns.source_pdf_quote:
...accomplish the goals of the planning study, then 
only one application needs to be submitted...
concerns.output_quote:
...accomplish the goals of the planning study, then
1 Levee Terms & Definitions (army.mil) only one application needs to be submitted...
concerns.location: End of Page 4 / Start of Page 5

concerns.description: Displaced hyperlinks: 3 link(s) extracted as standalone elements instead of inline text
concerns.analysis: standalone_links=3, examples=['https://deq.nc.gov/about/divisions/water-infrastructure/i-ne', 'https://deq.nc.gov/about/divisions/water-infrastructure/i-ne', 'https://www.epa.gov/G3/learn-about-green-streets']. Links should be inline within paragraphs, not separate blocks.

concerns.description:
Header/footer elements retained in output (1): LASII Stormwater Program Questions and Answers
LASII Program
concerns.analysis: header_footer_count=1. Header/footer text from extraction JSON is present in the rendered HTML — the renderer failed to strip it. This is a structural accessibility issue.
The source document uses underlining for emphasis in several places, which is not captured or preserved in the JSON output.

Page 2, Question 9: "why the Applicant considers it qualified as critical infrastructure."
concerns.output_quote:

Page 2: "why the Applicant considers it qualified as critical infrastructure."
